steps.title: Identify the antecedent
steps.content: The blank fills "______ compelling narrative still captivates readers today." The pronoun must modify "narrative" and refer to "The Aeneid" — a singular, non-person noun. The pronoun needs to be possessive.

steps.title: Rule: use "its" for the singular possessive of non-persons
steps.content:
Per-choice diagnosis:

A. "their" — plural possessive; "The Aeneid" is singular. Incorrect.

B. "they're" — contraction of "they are"; substitution gives "they are compelling narrative," which is ungrammatical. Incorrect.

C. "its" — singular possessive for non-persons. Substitution: "The Aeneid's compelling narrative still captivates readers" — meaning holds. Correct.

D. "it's" — contraction of "it is"; substitution gives "it is compelling narrative," which is ungrammatical as a possessive modifier. Incorrect.

C is correct.
